_note: Single source of truth for all numeric claims used across multiple documents. Update values HERE; run ci/sync_canonical_numbers.py to propagate. Agents must never hand-edit README.md / PR_DESCRIPTION.md / BACKEND_EVIDENCE.md numbers directly — always update this file first.
_canonical_profile_manifest_note: Single source of truth for deployment profiles (CMake preset + CAAS profile + enabled/disabled module set). Validated by ci/profile_manifest.py. Profile-related docs should pull names and module lists from this manifest; never hand-list module-on/off in BUILD_INTEGRATION_GUIDE.md / BITCOIN_CORE_INTEGRATION.md / README.md.
_generated: 2026-05-23 — machine-generated by bench_unified --json (generated_by field present). Run: taskset -c 0 nice -20 bench_unified --json. Turbo status: disabled (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, governor=performance, sudo-pinned). Replaces 2026-05-21 artifact which had turbo=unknown.

bitcoin_core_compat.note: 749/749 is the current GCC 14 run (docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json). An earlier Clang 19 run against v28.0 showed 693/693 (docs/BITCOIN_CORE_TEST_RESULTS.json).

connectblock.turbo_lock_status: CONFIRMED: cpupower frequency-set -g performance + intel_pstate/no_turbo=1 (2026-05-12 hard-turbo-locked rerun)
connectblock.wording: ConnectBlock aggregate block validation: +0.9–1.5% faster than libsecp256k1 with Release+LTO (GCC 14.2.0, hard turbo lock, 5 runs, err% 0.2–0.5%). Without LTO: ~0.5–1.0% slower (i-cache pressure; PERF-002 reduced gap from ~1.1% but did not eliminate it). LTO is required for Ultra to win all ConnectBlock workloads.
connectblock.wording_lto_range: **+0.9% to +1.5% faster** than libsecp256k1, confirmed (err% 0.2–0.5%, hard turbo lock: intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, 2026-05-12).
connectblock.wording_no_lto: Without LTO: ~0.5–1.0% slower than libsecp256k1 (AllEcdsa −0.5%, AllSchnorr −1.0%, Mixed −0.8%; measured 2026-05-12, hard turbo lock, 3 runs). PERF-002 reduced the gap from ~1.1% but did not eliminate it. Use Release+LTO for production Bitcoin Core builds.
connectblock.wording_bench_note: bench_bitcoin, Release+LTO, GCC 14.2.0, i5-14400F, hard turbo lock (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1), taskset -c 0, nice -20, 2026-05-12

taproot_signing.wording: Taproot signing: 10–35% faster than libsecp256k1 (SignTransactionSchnorr 1.10×; SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot 1.35×, GCC 14.2.0, Release+LTO, hard turbo lock, 2026-05-12, docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json).

taproot_verify.wording: P2TR script-path verification is ~10% faster (1.10×, VerifyScriptP2TR_ScriptPath, GCC 14.2.0 LTO).

schnorr_sign_merkle.wording: SignSchnorrWithMerkleRoot is ~35% faster (1.35×, GCC 14.2.0 LTO, hard turbo lock, docs/BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json).

ct_signing_clang19.wording: CT signing Clang 19 (archived 2026-03-24): 1.20–1.33× faster than libsecp256k1 (CT-vs-CT, not a current controlled run).

ct_signing_gcc.wording: CT signing GCC 14.2.0: 1.27–1.32× faster than libsecp256k1 (CT Schnorr 1.27×; CT ECDSA 1.32×; CT-vs-CT co-measured, bench_unified_2026-05-23_gcc14_x86-64.json). Turbo lock: CONFIRMED (intel_pstate/no_turbo=1, governor=performance, taskset -c 0 nice -20). Results are warm-cache (500 warmup/op, 11 passes IQR); see artifact for methodology.

_banned_patterns._note: These patterns must NEVER appear in reviewer-facing docs. check_bench_doc_consistency.py enforces this.

Stale no-LTO deficit — actual is ~1% not 2.5-5.4%
Stale taproot signing claim — actual is 11-36%
Stale P2TR verify claim — actual is ~11% (1.11x)
FAST-path sign ratio — variable-time vs libsecp CT, not production-equivalent, must not appear in reviewer docs
FAST-path sign ratio — variable-time vs libsecp CT, not production-equivalent, must not appear in reviewer docs

fuzz_corpus.wording: hundreds of thousands of deterministic corpus adversarial checks (count grows with CI runs; see audit/test_exploit_kat_corpus.cpp)
fuzz_corpus.summary_table_cell: Wycheproof, fault injection, hundreds of thousands of corpus checks (grows with CI)

gpu_throughput._note: Legacy umbrella block — superseded by silent_payments_btc / silent_payments_ltc / rpa_bch below. Kept for the ecdsa_signs_m_per_s field which still has no per-feature home. Will be removed after a controlled GPU ECDSA bench produces a canonical artifact.
gpu_throughput.status: diagnostic — no canonical JSON artifact; unverified against current GPU build
gpu_throughput.wording: [diagnostic/unverified — GPU ECDSA throughput: see GPU_BACKEND_EVIDENCE.json for methodology; numbers require re-run against current build before citing]
gpu_throughput.note: GPU ECDSA performance is out of scope for the Bitcoin Core PR evaluation profile.

silent_payments_btc._note: Bitcoin Silent Payments (BIP-352) scan + pipeline throughput. Source-of-truth: the two canonical bench artifacts below. Top-level numbers are extracted FROM those artifacts and propagated to docs via sync_canonical_numbers.py. Do not edit numbers here without first updating the artifact JSON.
silent_payments_btc._status: canonical (CPU + CUDA GPU); OpenCL and Metal pending controlled runs
silent_payments_btc.wording: BIP-352 scan throughput: CPU **13.7k tx/s (1T)** → **91k tx/s (16T)** on i5-14400F (artifact: `docs/bench_silent_payments_cpu_2026-05-19_i514400F.json`). GPU CUDA pipeline **10.96 M/s** (LUT, 91.2 ns/op) or **56.18 M/s** (LUT+PreSer, 17.8 ns/op) on RTX 5060 Ti (artifact: `benchmarks/gpu/cuda/rtx-50xx/bip352_rtx5060ti_20260504.txt`). OpenCL and Metal pipeline numbers pending controlled runs.
silent_payments_btc.note: BIP-352 (Silent Payments) is out of scope for the Bitcoin Core PR evaluation profile but tracked here for the multi-coin / GPU profile.

silent_payments_ltc._note: Litecoin Silent Payments scan throughput. Same secp256k1 primitives as BIP-352 BTC; LtcSpScanner precomputes spend_pubkey in constructor (closer to production wallet usage), which is why LTC-SP shows ~2.1x BTC throughput per-call.
silent_payments_ltc._status: canonical (CPU only); GPU pending controlled runs (estimated 5.38 M tx/s on RTX 5060 Ti per CPU artifact mainnet_context)

cpu_scan_tx_per_s.api: LtcSpScanner::scan_tx() with spend_pubkey precomputed in constructor

silent_payments_ltc.wording: Litecoin SP scan throughput: CPU **28.8k tx/s (1T)** → **192k tx/s (16T)** on i5-14400F (artifact: `docs/bench_silent_payments_cpu_2026-05-19_i514400F.json`). ~2.1× BTC BIP-352 per-call due to spend_pubkey precomputation in `LtcSpScanner`. GPU throughput: not yet measured — benchmark required.
silent_payments_ltc.note: Litecoin SP is out of scope for the Bitcoin Core PR evaluation profile.

rpa_bch._note: Bitcoin Cash Reusable Payment Address (RPA) scan + grinding throughput. Two canonical artifacts: a focused RPA-only bench (2026-05-18) and the unified Silent-Payments CPU bench (2026-05-19) which includes BCH RPA alongside BTC and LTC for direct comparison.
rpa_bch._status: canonical (CPU scan + grinding); GPU estimated only

cpu_scan_tx_per_s.api: RpaScanner::scan_tx() with spend_epk precomputed in constructor (lift_x cached since 2026-05-19 fix)
cpu_scan_tx_per_s._note_dual_artifact: focused bench (20k/153k) and unified bench (13.9k/97k) differ because the focused bench omits some scan validation steps that the unified bench includes for cross-chain parity.

rpa_bch._gpu_note: GPU scan and grinding throughput: not yet measured — benchmark required. Extrapolations from ct_ecdsa_sign_batch are banned per CLAUDE.md.
rpa_bch.wording: BCH RPA scan throughput: CPU **13.9k tx/s (1T)** → **97k tx/s (16T)** on i5-14400F (unified bench, artifact: `docs/bench_silent_payments_cpu_2026-05-19_i514400F.json`); focused RPA bench shows **20k tx/s (1c)** → **153k tx/s (16c)** with reduced cross-chain validation overhead (artifact: `docs/bench_bch_rpa_2026-05-18_gcc14_x86-64.json`). Grinding: **78k attempts/s (1c)** → **590k/s (16c)**. GPU throughput: not yet measured — benchmark required.
rpa_bch.note: BCH RPA is out of scope for the Bitcoin Core PR evaluation profile.

_note_exploit_counts: Dynamic counts (exploit PoC, modules) live in docs/canonical_data.json, generated by ci/build_canonical_data.py. Never duplicate them here.

evidence_commit._note: Full SHA of the commit at which the canonical benchmark evidence was recorded. Populated from BITCOIN_CORE_BENCH_RESULTS.json.backend_commit. Sync rule in ci/sync_canonical_numbers.py populates git-checkout lines in PR_BODY.md. Updated 2026-05-22: benchmark evidence at 48e7c02f (2026-05-12); P1 security fixes through f6b92035 (2026-05-22).

shim_microbench._note: Per-function shim microbenchmarks (secp256k1_* call path, NOT bench_unified primitive). Pending full controlled run including shim overhead. Update here; ci/sync_canonical_numbers.py will propagate to all docs.
shim_microbench._status: pending — run bench_unified --shim mode after all P1/P2 fixes are committed
shim_microbench.wording: [pending — shim microbenchmarks will be added after all P1/P2 fixes and a controlled bench_unified --shim run]

t11_schnorr_cache_first._note: BENCH-005: T-11 Schnorr verify cache-first optimization (commit 2cc88ba4).
t11_schnorr_cache_first.claim_basis: Derived from the known build_schnorr_verify_tables GLV table-build cost on bench_unified (which uses a 64-key warm pool). NOT measured via a controlled before/after shim-mode bench run with CPU pinning, turbo lock, and >=5 invocations. Value is an unverified_estimate_ns, not a measured result.
t11_schnorr_cache_first.promote_to_release_grade_when: A bench_unified --shim controlled run populates the per-function ns fields above with non-null values and the ci/check_bench_doc_consistency gate accepts the delta.

schnorr_verify_cold._note: BENCH-003: cold-start Schnorr verify (secp256k1_xonly_pubkey_parse + schnorr_verify in the same call, no pre-warmed pubkey cache). Measures the first-call cost a new pubkey faces — lift_x + GLV table build + dual_scalar_mul_gen_prebuilt.
schnorr_verify_cold._artifact_note: Supplementary turbo-enabled run. Turbo was NOT locked (intel_pstate/no_turbo not set). Use docs/bench_unified_2026-05-23_gcc14_x86-64.json for canonical CT-ratio claims; this artifact is only cited for the cold-start row.
schnorr_verify_cold.wording: Schnorr verify cold-start (parse + verify, no pre-warmed cache): 24795 ns on i5-14400F, GCC 14.2.0 Release+LTO, turbo enabled (supplementary run, docs/bench_unified_2026-05-27_gcc14_x86-64.json). Cached-xonly path: 25289 ns. libsecp256k1 warm: 25547 ns. Cold-start penalty vs cached: negligible at this granularity (turbo-enabled run; for CT-ratio claims, use the hard-turbo-locked 2026-05-23 artifact).
schnorr_verify_cold.do_not_quote_ratio_note: Turbo-enabled run is not comparable to the turbo-locked CT-ratio measurements. Cold vs warm delta is also within turbo jitter.

primitives_gcc._note: Ultra primitive arithmetic absolute timings (ns), GCC 14.2.0, turbo-locked. From the canonical artifact — never hand-edit; update the artifact + rerun.

sign_verify_gcc._note: Ultra FAST (variable-time, non-CT) sign/verify absolute timings (ns), GCC 14.2.0 turbo-locked. FAST sign is NOT production-equivalent to libsecp CT signing; reviewer ratios use ct_signing_gcc.

ct_signing_gcc_detail._note: CT signing absolute timings (ns) + CT-vs-FAST overhead. Companion to ct_signing_gcc (headline CT-vs-libsecp-CT ratios 1.32/1.27).

protocols_gcc._note: Protocol-level headline absolute timings (ns), GCC 14.2.0 turbo-locked.

batch_verify_gcc._note: Per-signature amortized batch-verify timings (ns), GCC 14.2.0 turbo-locked.

_note_module_counts_moved: Module counts (exploit_poc_count, non_exploit_modules, total_modules) used to live here as static fields. They have been removed in favour of docs/canonical_data.json, which is regenerated by ci/build_canonical_data.py from the authoritative audit/unified_audit_runner.cpp ALL_MODULES[] array. Carrying static copies here led to drift (265/114/379 here vs 269/122/391 there); single source of truth wins.
